commit.message:
Fix CRUD to match official SDK protocol (PUT + If-Not-Match)

The Dev Tunnels API requires:
- createTunnel: PUT /tunnels/{id} with If-Not-Match:* (not POST)
- updateTunnel: PUT /tunnels/{id} with If-Match:*
- createTunnelPort: PUT with If-Not-Match:*

Tunnel IDs are generated client-side using the same format as the
TS/Go SDKs: {adjective}-{noun}-{7 random chars}. Retries on 409
Conflict with a new ID (up to 3 attempts).

This fixes 401 errors on create that occurred because POST to
/tunnels is not a valid API endpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update gh-aw to v0.68.0 to fix MCP server blocking (#38089)

## Problem

Agentic workflow review agents (Architecture Review, Security Review,
etc.) were silently failing because Copilot CLI v1.0.22 blocked the
`github` and `safeoutputs` MCP servers at container startup:

```
! 2 MCP servers were blocked by policy: 'github', 'safeoutputs'
```

This caused agents to:
- Fall back to raw `git diff` commands instead of using GitHub API tools
(wasting tokens)
- Be unable to post review comments via
`create_pull_request_review_comment` (tool does not exist)
- Report **success** despite producing zero output (`agent_output.json:
{"items":[]}`)

**Example**: [Architecture Review run
24220062323](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/24220062323)
on PR #37993 consumed 2.94M tokens across 66 turns but posted zero
review comments.

## Root Cause

Tracked in github/gh-aw#25550 — fixed in gh-aw v0.67.4+.

## Changes

- `github/gh-aw-actions/setup`: v0.67.3 → v0.68.0
- `github/gh-aw/actions/setup`: v0.67.3 → v0.68.0
- `actions/github-script`: v8 → v9.0.0 (SHA-pinned)
- `mgmt-review.lock.yml`: strict mode enabled (compiler default)
- All 9 workflows recompiled with latest compiler

## Verification

After merge, re-trigger any review agent on a PR with the appropriate
label and confirm:
- No `MCP servers were blocked by policy` message in agent logs
- Review comments are successfully posted to the PR

---------

Co-authored-by: Deyaaeldeen Almahallawi <deyaa@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: azure-sdk-for-js

For consumers of the SDK we recommend visiting our public developer docs at https://docs.microsoft.com/javascript/azure/ or our versioned developer docs at https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk-for-js.

commit.message:
perf(interp): inline IC fast path for LdaGlobal/StaGlobal in SMI loop

Add raw-pointer slot access for global variable reads/writes in the
SMI fast loop. On IC hit, LdaGlobal reads directly from GlobalEnv.slots
bypassing RefCell borrow + HashMap lookup. StaGlobal writes directly
to slots with deferred sync_slots_to_vars() flush before function calls
and loop exit.

Also adds GlobalEnv::sync_slots_to_vars() to copy slot values back
into the vars HashMap when leaving the fast path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: corregir bugs criticos en scripts Relay (Host header WinRM + azbridge v0.16.1)

- Add-RelayClient.ps1:
  * HostName -> Host (campo correcto en azbridge v0.16.1 RemoteForward YAML)
  * HostPort ahora usa bindPort (no WinRMPort) para que Host header coincida
  * bindPort calculado ANTES de generar el YAML del cliente
  * localAddress (127.0.0.X unico) almacenado en server-registry.json
  * BindAddress del servidor usa el nombre del cliente (hostname alias)
  * Entrada en hosts file añadida automaticamente al registrar cliente

- Register-RelayClient.ps1:
  * WinRM se reconfigura al puerto correcto (HostPort del YAML)
  * azbridge usa CLI args (-x connStr -T relay:host:port) en vez de YAML
    para compatibilidad con azbridge v0.16.1 que rechaza Host: en YAML
  * Regla de firewall añadida automaticamente para el puerto WinRM

- Connect-RelaySession.ps1:
  * Usa hostname del cliente (no localhost) como ComputerName
  * Verifica hosts file y anade entrada si falta
  * Test-NetConnection en lugar de netstat para verificar el tunel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(maglev): increase deopt retry limit to prevent deep_object regression

The reduction from 1M to 100 retries caused deep_object_access_1k to
regress from ~3µs to 821µs because Maglev deopts during nested object
creation setup and exhausts the limit during criterion warmup. With the
benchmark binary split already preventing cross-contamination, a higher
limit is safe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use locale-independent date format in Windows build scripts

Windows date /t command returns locale-specific format (e.g., Chinese returns
周五04/10/2026, breaking the token parsing). Switch to PowerShell:
  powershell -Command "Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMddHHmm'"

This returns ISO 8601 format (yyyyMMddHHmm) independent of system locale.
Fallback to 'unknown' if PowerShell unavailable.

Fixes: cpp_cli.exe --version now shows correct format like '20260410-652a84b'
instead of '周五04101150-652a84b'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: windows build.bat now injects version info like CMake does

- Extract BUILD_TIME from system date/time (format: YYYYMMDDHHMM)
- Extract GIT_COMMIT_ID via 'git rev-parse --short HEAD'
- Pass both as /D compiler flags to cl.exe (BUILD_TIME and GIT_COMMIT_ID)
- Falls back to 'unknown' if git unavailable on Windows
- Updated build_debug.bat with same logic
- Now 'build.bat' produces binaries with --version support on Windows

Usage:
  build.bat
  build.bat --static
  build\cpp_cli.exe --version
  build\cpp_srv.exe --version

commit.message:
docs: Clarify `CreateNewSave` non-mutating behavior (#247)

`SaveManager.CreateNewSave` documentation claimed it sets `CurrentSave`,
but the implementation intentionally returns a new `SaveFile` without
mutating active state. This change updates docs and tests to match the
intended contract.

- **API contract docs**
- Updated `CreateNewSave` XML docs to explicitly state it returns a new
save and does **not** update `CurrentSave`.
- Removed an obsolete XML param doc that no longer matches the method
signature.

- **Unit test expectation**
  - Updated `SaveManager_CreatesNewSave` to assert:
    - a `SaveFile` is returned
    - `CurrentSave` remains unchanged after calling `CreateNewSave`

```csharp
var originalCurrentSave = saveManager.CurrentSave;
var createdSave = saveManager.CreateNewSave();

createdSave.ShouldNotBeNull();
saveManager.CurrentSave.ShouldBeSameAs(originalCurrentSave);
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HumanoidSandvichDispenser <25856867+HumanoidSandvichDispenser@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(friendly-python): soften absolute rules into default preferences with exceptions

Convert 5 absolute prohibitions into "strict default + documented exceptions":

1. TYPE_CHECKING: Prefer direct imports; allow TYPE_CHECKING for
   typing-only imports, expensive imports, and third-party cycles
2. Re-export: Avoid in application code; allow in library public API
   with __all__
3. Empty __init__.py: Package strategy must be explicit — regular
   packages (with __init__.py) and namespace packages are both valid
4. Pyright venvPath/venv: Distinguish CLI Pyright (venvPath+venv)
   from Pylance (interpreter selection)
5. New §10: Boundary layer exceptions — wider types allowed at system
   edges (API, ORM, plugins, prototypes), must narrow before core domain

Updated: SKILL.md (sections, checklist, frontmatter),
         advanced-patterns.md (circular imports section + Option 4)
Added:   design document in docs/plans/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle backslash-escaped quotes in _split_params

Track escape state so that backslash-escaped quote characters inside
string defaults (e.g. "he said \"hi,bye\"") do not prematurely end
the quoted region, which would cause incorrect comma splitting.

Adds test_wrap_default_with_escaped_quote_and_comma.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[development-frontend-dev-c203] Add error handling components: ErrorBoundary, ErrorFallback, useAsyncError hook

- ErrorBoundary: React class component that catches render errors with
  customizable fallback UI, onError callback, and reset/retry support
- ErrorFallback: Reusable error display with severity levels (error/warning),
  retry button, secondary action, inline mode, and ARIA role=alert
- useAsyncError: Hook for async operations with loading/error/success states,
  automatic retry with exponential backoff, error normalization
- DashboardPage: Added error and onRetry props for error state rendering
- Updated all index.ts exports for new components and hook
- 78 tests passing (13 ErrorFallback + 8 ErrorBoundary + 10 useAsyncError + 47 Dashboard)

commit.message:
Fix duplicate finder: paginate results and fix rendering during scan

  doesn't try to render 392+ groups while the spinner is still visible
- Paginate results: show 20 groups at a time with 'Show more' button
- Increase max-height to 60vh for better usability
- Reset visible count when opening a new scan

The root cause was that rendering hundreds of groups with nested word
info, badges, and buttons in a single Blazor diff overwhelmed the
browser, making it appear frozen even though the scan had completed.

Add \\uhhhh unicode escape support to parser (#142)

Parse \\uhhhh (exactly 4 hex digits) in regex patterns, matching JDK
behavior. When a high surrogate is followed by a \\uhhhh low surrogate,
they are combined into a single supplementary code point.

Fixes #133

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#27): set contentWindowInsets=0 on Scaffold to fix IME gap on Android 12+

On API 30+ with enableEdgeToEdge(), adjustResize is ignored. Scaffold's default
contentWindowInsets consumes the bottom inset before imePadding() can act on it.
Setting WindowInsets(0) lets imePadding() receive the full IME inset correctly.
Also restore navigationBarsPadding() on InputBar and add outlineVariant border
to fenced code blocks for clearer visual separation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: kernel-ai-assistant
repository.description: Kernel AI Assistant is a high-performance, local-first intelligent agent designed to bridge the gap between cloud-scale reasoning and total data sovereignty. Built specifically for the Android ecosystem, Kernel AI bypasses the cloud entirely, executing state-of-the-art Gemma-4 E-4B and E-2B models directly on your device's NPU and GPU.

commit.message:
Funnel: single-row horizontal bars, drastically smaller

Bars are now single horizontal line: value + percentages side-by-side
(e.g. '3.6M  60% · 30%' all on one row)
- Bar height: ~12px (was ~18px with stacked text)
- Font: 7px value, 6px percentages, 6px labels
- Labels width: 40px
- Gap between bars: 1px
- 7 bars × 12px = ~84px total content height

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: copilot-exec-dashboard
repository.description: GitHub Copilot Executive Dashboard - interactive HTML dashboard

commit.message:
Fix hooks to official GitHub Copilot CLI specification

Refs: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/use-hooks
Refs: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/hooks-configuration
Refs: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/tutorials/copilot-cli-hooks

Structure fixes:
- hooks.json: 'hooks' key is now an object (not array) keyed by event name
- Each hook entry uses 'type','bash'/'powershell','cwd','timeoutSec','comment' per spec
- Removed unsupported 'event' and 'script' keys
- Added 'version: 1' was already present; kept
- cwd set to '.github/hooks' so script paths resolve correctly

Script fixes — all now read stdin JSON via official PS protocol:
  \ = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd() | ConvertFrom-Json
- session-start.ps1: reads source + initialPrompt from input
- post-build-read-output.ps1: reads toolName/toolArgs/toolResult; skips if not a build
- error-capture.ps1: reads error.message/name/stack from input

New hooks added (per official event types):
- userPromptSubmitted: log-prompt.ps1 — logs redacted prompts to audit.jsonl
- preToolUse: pre-tool-policy.ps1 — blocks dangerous ops, warns on D365 duty/role changes

New files:
- .github/hooks/scripts/log-prompt.ps1
- .github/hooks/scripts/pre-tool-policy.ps1
- .github/hooks/logs/.gitkeep (directory placeholder)

.gitignore: add .github/hooks/logs/ exclusion (local audit logs not committed)

Updated copilot-instructions.md and using-d365-fo-skills/SKILL.md:
- correct script paths (.github/hooks/scripts/)
- full 5-hook table with all event types
- note that scripts use official stdin JSON protocol

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: keep hidden teams visible in workspace roster (#23)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix funnel height: drastically shrink bars, single-line percentages

Reverted grid-auto-rows (broke top row). Instead shrunk funnel content:
- Bar font: 8px (was 10), padding: 1px (was 3), border-radius: 3px
- Labels: 7px (was 8), width 48px (was 55)
- Combined '% of prior' and '% of total' into single line
- Removed placeholder lines from Page Visits bar (was 3 lines, now 1)
- Each bar is now ~18px tall instead of ~40px
- 7 bars × 18px = ~126px total, fits within chart card height

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
